title: Anger at funeral of Lebanese journalist killed by Israeli army: 'Amal gave voice to the south and the sacrifices endured by this region'
subhead: Shortly before Donald Trump announced on Thursday a three-week extension of the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, friends and relatives of Amal Khalil were burying the reporter, who had been killed by an Israeli strike the previous day.

title: The political left is disappearing across Central Europe, despite Orban's defeat in Hungary
Weather sensors at Charles de Gaulle Airport at center of suspected manipulation on Polymarket
French government unveils urgent bill with measures on urban renewal and energy-inefficient homes
subhead: Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu announced a series of measures set to become a bill to be presented by summer.

title: White House confirms US envoys will head to Pakistan for new rounds of talks with Iran
subhead: Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will depart for Pakistan on Saturday for a second round of talks with Iran. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has already arrived in Islamabad ahead of the meeting.

title: Claude Bessy, star dancer and director of the Paris Opéra's dance school, dies at 93
subhead: Born into a family of artists in 1932, Claude Bessy, who devoted her life to dance and trained several generations of dancers, died in Paris on Thursday, April 23, at the age of 93.

title: From 'The Sims' to 'The Last of Us': How video games allow gamers to explore their sexuality
subhead: Freed from inhibition by the anonymity of the digital world, some gamers find liberation both for their avatars and themselves, leading them to coming out, transitioning or experimenting with their sexuality 'IRL.'

Jewish settlers in West Bank escalate harassment and terror campaign to annex Palestinian land
subhead: The death of a 14-year-old boy shot dead on Tuesday outside his school in Al-Mughayyir marks a new peak in an unprecedented spiral of violence in the West Bank. Targeted killings, destruction of farmland and expanding settlements have all intensified, largely ignored by political and diplomatic actors.

title: Historian Pascal Blanchard: 'Identity will be one of the main themes of France's presidential campaign'
subhead: The issue of identity, tackled head-on by both the far-right and the radical left, calls for political solutions to heal divisions as the 2027 presidential election approaches, explains Pascal Blanchard, an expert in colonization, in an interview.

title: In Lebanon, Israel establishes an uninhabited buffer zone behind a 'yellow line'
subhead: The 'demarcation line' drawn by Israel on the third day of the ceasefire has revived memories of the 18-year occupation of Lebanon from 1982 to 2000. The Israeli army has banned Lebanese citizens from entering this 'defense zone,' which covers 6% of the country.
